meta.summary: ByteDance Seed launched Seed 2.0 and introduced a Full-Duplex Speech LLM aimed at more natural real-time voice interaction, alongside a Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview release on Arena. DeepSeek cut input-cache-hit pricing across its entire API lineup to 10% of prior levels while keeping a 75% discount live for DeepSeek-V4-Pro, signaling continued price pressure in model serving. On the research side, the HGQ-LUT paper proposed faster LUT-aware training for FPGA inference, while a separate constrained-LQR paper reported near-optimal regret guarantees for safe adaptive control.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: Near-Optimal Regret for the Safe Learning-based Control of the Constrained Linear Quadratic Regulator
items.summary_short: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

topic_refs.headline: Constrained-LQR Paper Reports Near-Optimal Regret for Safe Adaptive Control

items.title: HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference
items.summary_short: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives.

topic_refs.headline: HGQ-LUT Paper Targets Faster LUT-Aware Training for FPGA DNN Inference

items.title: SOC-ICNN: From Polyhedral to Conic Geometry for Learning Convex Surrogate Functions
items.summary_short: Classical ReLU-based Input Convex Neural Networks (ICNNs) are equivalent to the optimal value functions of Linear Programming (LP). This intrinsic structural equivalence restricts their representational capacity to piecewise-linear polyhedral functions.

topic_refs.headline: SOC-ICNN: From Polyhedral to Conic Geometry for Learning Convex Surrogate Functions

items.title: Shared Lexical Task Representations Explain Behavioral Variability In LLMs
items.summary_short: One of the most common complaints about large language models (LLMs) is their prompt sensitivity -- that is, the fact that their ability to perform a task or provide a correct answer to a question can depend unpredictably on the way the question is posed.

topic_refs.headline: Shared Lexical Task Representations Explain Behavioral Variability In LLMs

items.title: Relaxation-Informed Training of Neural Network Surrogate Models
items.summary_short: ReLU neural networks trained as surrogate models can be embedded exactly in mixed-integer linear programs (MILPs), enabling global optimization over the learned function.

topic_refs.headline: Relaxation-Informed Training of Neural Network Surrogate Models

items.title: Dissociating Decodability and Causal Use in Bracket-Sequence Transformers
items.summary_short: This paper examines the gap between what transformer representations make decodable and what the model causally uses during computation. It focuses on bracket-sequence transformers and uses interventions on residual subspaces and attention behavior

topic_refs.headline: Dissociating Decodability and Causal Use in Bracket-Sequence Transformers

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Shows a clean decodability-versus-causal-use gap in Dyck transformers by intervening on residual subspaces and stack-like attention.

items.title: How LLMs Detect and Correct Their Own Errors: The Role of Internal Confidence Signals
items.summary_short: This paper investigates how large language models detect and correct their own mistakes using internal confidence signals. It is a mechanistic interpretability study focused on self-correction behavior

topic_refs.headline: How LLMs Detect and Correct Their Own Errors: The Role of Internal Confidence Signals

items.spotlight_comment: Mechanistic interpretability result on self-correction in frontier LLMs is likely of broad day-level interest.

items.title: Universal Transformers Need Memory: Depth-State Trade-offs in Adaptive Recursive Reasoning
items.summary_short: The explicit claim that adaptive universal transformers need internal scratchpad memory for recursive reasoning points toward a potentially important design direction
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
items.spotlight_comment: The explicit claim that adaptive universal transformers need internal scratchpad memory for recursive reasoning points toward a potentially important design direction.

items.title: Hidden Failure Modes of Gradient Modification under Adam in Continual Learning, and Adaptive Decoupled Moment Routing as a Repair
items.summary_short: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient modification methods fail under Adam through second-moment dynamics and proposes decoupled moment routing as a fix
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient modification methods fail under Adam through second-moment dynamics and proposes decoupled moment routing as a fix.

items.title: SpikingBrain2.0: Brain-Inspired Foundation Models for Efficient Long-Context and Cross-Platform Inference
items.summary_short: Large long-context foundation-model architecture with substantial efficiency claims and broad interest beyond the specialized list
items.spotlight_comment: Large long-context foundation-model architecture with substantial efficiency claims and broad interest beyond the specialized list.

items.title: Kernel Contracts: A Specification Language for ML Kernel Correctness Across Heterogeneous Silicon
items.summary_short: The contract-based formalization of ML kernel correctness across silicon looks like a new systems direction rather than routine optimization work
items.spotlight_comment: The contract-based formalization of ML kernel correctness across silicon looks like a new systems direction rather than routine optimization work.

items.title: Contexts are Never Long Enough: Structured Reasoning for Scalable Question Answering over Long Document Sets
items.summary_short: Long-context QA over document sets is broadly timely, and the structured-state approach is likely to draw attention beyond the niche memory-systems audience

items.spotlight_comment: Long-context QA over document sets is broadly timely, and the structured-state approach is likely to draw attention beyond the niche memory-systems audience.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Ships Seed 2.0 and Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for more natural voice interaction. The model is described as supporting attentive listening and robust interference suppression
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is pushing beyond text models into real-time voice agents, a strategic signal for companies evaluating multimodal assistant platforms and speech-native interfaces.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 and separately released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating both a production model push and an early-access evaluation path.
The new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM is positioned around attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking, targeting a key weakness in current voice assistants.
All three announcements came from ByteDance Seed directly, giving this cluster stronger signal than third-party commentary or benchmark reposts.

evidence.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview released on Arena
Seed 2.0 Official Launch

featured_topics.headline: DeepSeek Cuts API Input Cache Hit Pricing by 90% Across Entire Model Line
featured_topics.summary_short: DeepSeek announced a price reduction for input cache hits across the entire DeepSeek API series. The new price is effective immediately and is set at one-tenth of the previous level
featured_topics.why_it_matters: A 10x reduction in cache-hit pricing can materially change inference economics for high-volume enterprise workloads and intensify pricing pressure on competing API vendors.
key_takeaways: DeepSeek said input cache hits across the entire DeepSeek API series now cost just 1/10th of the previous price, effective immediately.
The company also said its DeepSeek-V4-Pro promotion remains active at 75% off, stacking a major temporary discount on top of the broader cache-pricing cut.
The move specifically rewards repeated-context and retrieval-heavy applications, where cache-hit rates can meaningfully lower total serving cost.

evidence.title: 🔥DeepSeek Input Cache Price Drop! Effective immediately, the price for input cache hits across the ENTIRE DeepSeek API series is reduced to just 1/10th of the original price! Bu...

featured_topics.headline: HGQ-LUT Paper Targets Faster LUT-Aware Training for FPGA DNN Inference
featured_topics.summary_short: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives.
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If validated, this could improve the practicality of FPGA-based AI deployment for latency- and power-sensitive edge and industrial systems.
key_takeaways: The paper, titled "HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference," focuses on lookup-table neural networks that map arithmetic directly onto FPGA logic primitives.
Its core claim is to address a major adoption bottleneck in LUT-aware training, which prior methods have struggled with due to complexity and limited usability in practice.
The work is aimed at ultra-low-latency, hardware-efficient inference, making it more relevant to edge deployment and custom accelerators than to mainstream GPU serving.

evidence.title: HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference

featured_topics.headline: Constrained-LQR Paper Reports Near-Optimal Regret for Safe Adaptive Control
featured_topics.summary_short: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step.
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Safe learning guarantees matter for robotics, autonomy, and industrial control systems where AI policies must satisfy hard constraints during deployment, not just in simulation.
key_takeaways: The paper studies stochastic linear quadratic regulator control with constraints that must hold at every time step, a stricter setting than unconstrained adaptive control.
It positions its result against prior multidimensional work showing roughly O(T^(2/3)) regret, framing the new contribution as a near-optimal improvement.
The emphasis on robust constraint satisfaction makes the result potentially relevant for safety-critical applications such as autonomous systems and process control.

evidence.title: Near-Optimal Regret for the Safe Learning-based Control of the Constrained Linear Quadratic Regulator
